terms.definition: Statistical test for stationarity in time series. A p-value < 0.05 indicates the signal segment is stationary, validating the use of mean/variance statistics for feature extraction.

Artifact where high-frequency signal components are incorrectly represented as lower frequencies after downsampling below the Nyquist rate. The main information loss mechanism at high compression ratios.
The instantaneous magnitude of a complex IQ signal (|I + jQ|), representing signal power variations over time. Used to detect burst patterns and compare drone temporal signatures.
Measure of similarity between a signal and a time-shifted version of itself. Used to find repeating patterns.
Frequency range occupied by a signal, typically measured in Hz or MHz. Different drone protocols use different bandwidths (e.g., 20MHz for WiFi).
Signal centered around 0 Hz (DC) before modulation onto a carrier frequency.
Software-defined radio hardware platform used to capture the DroneDetect V2 dataset at 60 MHz sampling rate in the 2.4 GHz ISM band.
Statistical method that estimates metric uncertainty by resampling predictions with replacement thousands of times. Provides 95% confidence intervals for accuracy and F1-score of each model.
Bandwidth (in MHz or frequency bins) containing 90% or 99% of the total signal energy. Used to characterize spectral occupancy and compare drone spectral footprints.
Central frequency used to transport information in RF communications.
Supervised learning task of assigning an input to one of several predefined categories. In this project, the task is identifying which of 7 drone models produced a given RF signal.
Deep learning architecture that automatically learns spatial patterns. Applied to spectrograms for end-to-end drone signal classification.
Unwanted signals occupying the same frequency band as the signal of interest. In this project, WiFi and Bluetooth transmissions overlapping with drone signals in the 2.4 GHz band.
Ratio of standard deviation to mean (std/mean), a dimensionless measure of relative variability. Used for envelope CV to compare amplitude variability across drones.
Standardized effect size measuring the difference between two group means in units of pooled standard deviation. Thresholds: small (0.2), medium (0.5), large (0.8).
64-bit complex number format where each sample consists of two 32-bit floats (I and Q). The native storage format for IQ data in the DroneDetect dataset.
Table showing model predictions versus actual labels for each class. Reveals specific classification errors and class-wise performance.
Model evaluation technique that partitions data into k subsets, training on k-1 and testing on the remaining one, repeated k times. Standard 5-fold CV used for quick benchmarks.
When test data information inadvertently influences model training, leading to overly optimistic performance estimates. Critical issue to avoid in time-series RF data.
Logarithmic unit for expressing power ratios: PSD_dB = 10 * log10(PSD_linear). Compresses the wide dynamic range of RF signals into a manageable scale. 10 dB = 10x power difference.
Unwanted constant component in a signal that shifts the baseline.
Frequency shift caused by relative motion between transmitter and receiver. Relevant for flying (FY) state where drone movement may introduce frequency shifts.

Reduction of the number of samples in a signal, lowering its effective sampling rate. RF-UAVNet requires a 120x downsampling factor, which significantly degrades signal fidelity and limits model performance.
Public dataset containing RF signals from various drone models and communication protocols. Includes both drone and background interference signals.
Communication technique that spreads the signal over a wider bandwidth using a pseudo-random code. Used by some drone communication protocols.
Training regularization technique that halts training when validation loss stops improving for a specified number of epochs (patience). Prevents overfitting without manual epoch tuning.
One complete pass through the entire training dataset during model training. Multiple epochs are needed for convergence; our CNNs train for up to 100 epochs with early stopping.
Harmonic mean of precision and recall, providing a single metric that balances both error types. Reported per-class and macro-averaged to evaluate model performance across all 7 drone classes.
Process of creating meaningful input variables from raw data for ML models. For RF signals, includes statistical, spectral, and temporal features.
Algorithm that converts time-domain signals into frequency-domain representation. Reveals the frequency components present in a signal.
Communication technique where the signal rapidly switches between frequency channels following a pseudo-random sequence. Used by some drone protocols to resist interference and improve security.
Data partitioning strategy where all segments from the same recording file stay in the same set (train, validation, or test). Our key contribution that prevents data leakage from correlated segments.
Technique where signal rapidly switches between frequency channels (e.g., Bluetooth uses 1600 hops/second).
The spacing between adjacent frequency bins in an FFT, equal to sampling_rate / NFFT. Determines the finest frequency detail observable in spectral analysis.
Bell-shaped window function that reduces spectral leakage by tapering signal edges before FFT. Used in our PSD and spectrogram computations with nperseg=1024.
Gain and phase mismatch between the In-phase and Quadrature channels caused by hardware imperfections. Acts as a device-specific RF fingerprint for drone identification.
The derivative of the unwrapped phase of a complex signal, converted to Hz. Reveals frequency modulation and frequency hopping patterns characteristic of different drone protocols.
In-phase and Quadrature components that represent complex RF signals, where I is the real part and Q is the imaginary part. Essential for capturing both amplitude and phase information.
Unlicensed radio frequency bands reserved for non-commercial use. The 2.4 GHz ISM band is where all drone signals in this project are captured.
DJI's earlier communication protocol for drone control and video transmission. Features distinct RF characteristics for detection.
Downsampling method that estimates new sample values by linearly interpolating between existing samples. Used in the RFUAVNet pipeline (np.interp) but introduces aliasing at high compression ratios.
Reference oscillator used for frequency conversion in RF receivers.

Non-parametric statistical test comparing two classifiers based on their disagreement patterns on the same test set. Used to determine if performance differences between our 4 models are statistically significant.
The baseline power level in a signal below which useful information cannot be detected. Interference raises the noise floor, reducing effective SNR.
Scaling of IQ signal samples to have zero mean and unit variance. Applied per-segment before feature extraction to remove DC offset and amplitude variations between recordings.
Maximum observable frequency, equal to half the sampling rate.
DJI's proprietary communication protocol operating in 2.4GHz and 5.8GHz bands. One of the drone communication standards used in the dataset.
Modulation scheme that splits data across many closely-spaced orthogonal subcarriers. Used by WiFi-based drones in the 2.4 GHz band, producing characteristic spectral signatures.
Property where two signals have zero correlation (dot product = 0).
Raw estimate of the power spectral density computed from a single FFT of the signal. The Welch method averages multiple overlapping periodograms to reduce variance.
Process of removing 2-pi discontinuities from the phase of a complex signal to obtain a continuous phase trajectory. Required before computing instantaneous frequency.
Vector representation of a sinusoidal quantity showing amplitude and phase.

Method for calibrating SVM decision function outputs into probabilities by fitting a logistic regression on the margins. Enables our SVM to produce comparable probability estimates alongside CNN softmax outputs.
Measures the power distribution of a signal across different frequencies. Used to identify characteristic frequency patterns in drone communication signals.
90-degree phase shift between two signals (the "Q" in IQ).
Ensemble machine learning method that builds multiple decision trees and aggregates their predictions. Used as a quick discriminative power benchmark across downsampling levels.
One of 5 repeated recordings (indexed 0-4) for each drone/state/interference combination in the DroneDetect V2 dataset. Used to assess intra-condition reproducibility.
Task of identifying signal types or sources from raw radio frequency measurements. Core objective of drone detection systems.
Specialized CNN architecture designed for RF-based drone detection. Uses multi-scale convolutional blocks optimized for spectrogram analysis.
Number of signal samples captured per second, measured in Hz. Determines the maximum frequency that can be accurately captured (Nyquist limit).
Radio system where signal processing is done in software rather than hardware.
A 20 ms slice of raw IQ data extracted from a recording file. Each file yields multiple non-overlapping segments that become independent samples for training and evaluation.
Ratio of signal power to noise power, expressed in decibels (dB). Higher SNR indicates cleaner signal reception and better classification conditions.

Activation function that converts a vector of raw scores into a probability distribution summing to 1. Used as the final layer of our CNN models (VGG16, ResNet50, RF-UAVNet) to output per-class probabilities.
The weighted mean frequency of a power spectrum, where weights are normalized PSD values. Indicates the center of gravity of the spectral energy distribution.
Measure of the peakedness of the power distribution across frequency bins. High kurtosis indicates energy concentrated in few frequency bands.
Frequency spreading artifact caused by non-ideal windowing in FFT analysis.
Measure of asymmetry in the power distribution across frequency bins. Positive skewness indicates spectral energy is skewed toward lower frequencies.
Standard deviation of the frequency distribution weighted by normalized PSD. Measures how broadly signal energy is distributed around the spectral centroid.
Visual representation showing how signal frequencies change over time. Created by computing FFT on sliding windows of the signal.
Property of a signal whose statistical properties (mean, variance) do not change over time. Validated at 20 ms segments via ADF test; required for reliable feature extraction.
Fourier transform applied to short overlapping windows of a signal, producing a time-frequency representation (spectrogram). Core transform in our CNN pipeline with 1024-sample windows.
Data partitioning approach that preserves class distribution proportions across train/validation/test sets. Essential for handling imbalanced drone datasets.
Classical ML algorithm that finds optimal hyperplanes to separate classes. Effective for RF signal classification with hand-crafted features.
Technique of using pre-trained model weights from one task as initialization for another. Reduces training time and data requirements.
Algorithm for estimating power spectral density by averaging periodograms from overlapping signal segments. Reduces noise in frequency analysis.
Mathematical function applied to signal segments before FFT to reduce spectral leakage. Common types include Hamming, Hanning, and Blackman windows.
